raw_consumer_statement:
What happened? I ordered auto parts from XXXX XXXX, XXXX XX/XX/year>. The parts arrived damaged, and I followed their return policy to have them returned promptly and correctly. 

XXXX XXXX, XXXX received them XX/XX/year>. The amount of the purchase was {$560.00}. XXXX XXXX XXXX phone representative acknowledged receiving them on the phone that day and the warehouse would take a few days to send it through the other side. A few more weeks went by and couldn't receive from XXXX XXXX XXXX. 

Then, on XX/XX/year> I disputed it with Citi Bank after I returned them ; XXXX XXXX XXXX received the parts they sent me. Citi temporarily credited my account. XX/XX/year> they reversed the credit. And apparently closed in XXXX XXXX XXXX favor. I reopened XX/XX/year> to dispute it, after Citi Bank sent me a letter asking ; " If I continued to dispute this charge please provide ; Proof you returned the merchandise. Examples of this proof are a postal receipt, tracking number, or a tracer from the shipping company. '' I provided these through email and letter through XXXX and tracked and it was received. 

Citi sided with the merchant and stated they have had no response from XXXX XXXX XXXX, confirming anything and as to why they will not refund the money. 

I also have audio recording to go along with Citi bank stating, they've had no response as to why I shouldn't receive credit. 


XX/XX/year> : You ordered from XXXX XXXX, XXXX. 

Parts arrived damaged, and you returned them following their policy. 

XX/XX/year> : XXXX XXXX confirmed receipt of the return. 

XX/XX/year> : You filed a dispute with Citi Bank after delays and no refund. 

XX/XX/year> : Citi reversed the temporary credit, siding with XXXX XXXX. 

XX/XX/year> : You reopened the dispute, sending proof of return ( e.g., tracking, delivery confirmation ). 

Citi acknowledged receipt of the documents, but as of now, the case remains unresolved.
